segments.title: Pearl Harbor planning joins secrecy and carrier mass
segments.summary: The Pearl Harbor planning lecture argues that Japanese carrier-strike planning combined earlier conceptual work, experienced air planners, secrecy, and massed carrier aviation.
segments.body: The lecture separates official 1941 operational planning from earlier carrier-strike thinking. Dr. Clarke highlights planners who tied logistics and force design together, then explains why secrecy, routing, and multi-carrier mass mattered to making the Pearl Harbor strike plausible.

evidence.note: The transcript distinguishes official 1941 planning from earlier carrier-strike discussions and identifies planners coordinating logistics, strategy, and carrier experience.
The Japanese concept of operations is framed around secrecy, route selection, multi-carrier operations, and using enough aircraft to improve strike accuracy.
